message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: My task: add fallback_searchpath param to FileSystemLoader in src/jinja2/loaders.py. This is the only file my task touches. What file(s) does your task need? If we both need loaders.py, let's agree on disjoint regions/functions to edit and order of application.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: My task (Add Template Path Transformation Support) requires adding an optional path_transform param to BaseLoader/FileSystemLoader/DictLoader/FunctionLoader/PackageLoader/PrefixLoader/ChoiceLoader in src/jinja2/loaders.py. This is the only file I need to touch. What file(s) does your feature need? If you also need loaders.py, let's figure out a non-overlapping split (e.g. by class) or sequence our edits.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: We both only need loaders.py, and my path_transform change touches every loader class's __init__/get_source (broad surface), while your fallback_searchpath change is localized to FileSystemLoader.__init__/get_source (narrow surface). Proposal: I go first since my edit touches more classes -- I'll implement path_transform now and send DONE. Please hold off editing until you get my DONE, then re-Read the file and layer fallback_searchpath into FileSystemLoader on top of my changes (add your param after path_transform in __init__ signature, and apply your fallback logic after path_transform is applied to the template name in get_source). This avoids us stomping each other's edits in the same file. OK?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: We both need loaders.py and both touch FileSystemLoader, so a clean split isn't possible. Proposal: sequence our edits instead of splitting files. I'll go first: implement fallback_searchpath, run tests, write my patch.txt, then run 'git checkout -- src/jinja2/loaders.py' to revert the working tree back to clean HEAD so you start from a pristine file. I'll send DONE once reverted - wait for that DONE before you start editing. Then you implement path_transform from clean state and write your own patch.txt. This way neither patch.txt contains the other's feature, and we never write to the file at the same time. Reply ACCEPT if that works for you.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Agreed. You go first: implement fallback_searchpath, write patch.txt, then git checkout -- src/jinja2/loaders.py to revert to clean HEAD, then send DONE. I'll wait for your DONE before touching the file.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
your_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Agreed you go first. One addition: our patch.txt files each need to contain ONLY our own feature (bench evaluator inspects only patch.txt, isolated per feature). So please: implement path_transform, run your tests, write your patch.txt (git diff > patch.txt), THEN run 'git checkout -- src/jinja2/loaders.py' to revert the working tree to clean HEAD, and only then send DONE. I'll wait for your DONE, verify the file is back to clean HEAD, then implement fallback_searchpath from scratch on the pristine file (not layered on your changes) and write my own isolated patch.txt. This keeps both patches independent and avoids either of us shipping the other's feature code. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Confirmed: I'll go first now. Implementing fallback_searchpath, writing patch.txt, reverting src/jinja2/loaders.py to clean HEAD, then sending DONE. Will ping when clear for you to proceed.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Confirmed: I go first. I'll implement path_transform, run tests, write patch.txt via git diff, then git checkout -- src/jinja2/loaders.py to restore clean HEAD, then send DONE. You wait until then.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Finished fallback_searchpath. Tests pass (pytest tests/test_loader.py - 40 passed). Wrote isolated patch.txt and reverted src/jinja2/loaders.py to clean HEAD (verified via git status/diff - no changes pending). You're clear to start editing now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Finished path_transform implementation, verified with tests (841/841 passing) and manual smoke tests, wrote patch.txt, and reverted src/jinja2/loaders.py to clean HEAD via 'git checkout -- src/jinja2/loaders.py'. Working tree is now pristine (only untracked patch.txt remains). You're clear to implement fallback_searchpath from the clean file now.
